expectations.text: Organizes output into the 5 canonical buckets (pain points, trigger moments, objections, transformations, standout language)
expectations.evidence: Organized by Core Insight/VOC Language/JTBD/Objections/Angles/Personas — not the 5 canonical buckets

expectations.text: References scoring reviews 1-5 for quality before analysis
expectations.evidence: No 1-5 scoring; only splits 'four substantive vs four short/generic' qualitatively

expectations.text: Surfaces actual quotes from reviews (not just summary)
expectations.evidence: 7 direct VOC quotes pulled e.g. 'grounding without being overpowering', 'calmer within five minutes'
No numeric 1-5 scoring applied to individual reviews — only substantive/short binary

expectations.text: Discards or deprioritizes the 1-2 scored reviews (e.g. 'Love it', 'Smells great', 'Nice scent')
expectations.evidence: Section 'What the Short Reviews Tell Us' calls them 'not creative fuel' and deprioritizes them

expectations.text: Highlights the breakup review and kitchen/chaos review as high-signal
expectations.evidence: Both featured as signature insights: breakup as Angle 3, kitchen chaos as Angle 2 with highest-performing labels
